finding_results.notes: Replaced interior-only 'You are thirty-four years old, Nate almost said' with spoken dialogue: Nate starts saying 'I'm thirty-four, I can—' aloud, catches the wrong number (his real age, not Dominic's 31), and corrects to 'Thirty-one' audibly. Victor's reaction at line 293 now specifies he sharpened on the number, not the argument ('not at the argument, at the number before it'). A cold reader can now state on first pass: Nate accidentally says his real age (34), corrects to Dominic's age (31), and Victor notices the numerical slip. The interior beat ('His age, not Dominic's') makes the identity-confusion stakes legible without over-explaining.

Two of three cited maxims roughened into context-dependent speech that cannot be lifted as standalone aphorisms. (1) Ray's line at 61: 'If he has to imagine himself as a good person first, you're dead' replaced with 'don't put him in a spot where he's gotta feel like a good person to say yes, 'cause he won't do it. He'll just shut it down' — rougher syntax, Ray-true contractions ('gotta', ''cause'), and referent-dependent phrasing that requires knowing who 'him' is. (2) Victor's line at 193: 'Nobody believes in mercy until they know where the cars go' replaced with 'put the parking count on page one. You had it buried. People aren't going to sit through the tenant argument until they see the lot works' — specific to the actual document Victor reviewed, depends on knowing what 'the tenant argument' and 'the lot' refer to. Also removed three sentences of narrator interpretation ('It might have been advice. It might have been a joke. Victor's tone gave him nothing to work with.') that restated what the scene already made legible. The third cited beat (snapshot line 297, 'Better office, nicer suit. Still a cog') had already been reworked in a prior pass to 'A cog, if you want the ugly word' at line 289, which is less quotable and more conversational.

summary: Both findings addressed. The age-slip at line 291 now reads as spoken dialogue where Nate audibly says the wrong age (34 instead of 31) and corrects, making the identity danger and Victor's sharpened attention legible on first pass. Two minted maxims (Ray's 'good person' line, Victor's 'mercy/parking' line) roughened into context-dependent speech that requires the surrounding exchange to land. No changes to heading contract, continuity, or chapter structure. Total net effect: three lines of narrator commentary removed, three dialogue lines rewritten with rougher texture.
